lines: Petch Marrow, wreck-picker. This wash ate a sandwyrm and half a caravan, and I mean to pick both clean before the wind reburies them.
Grab that rope end. One good lash on the rib sled and the road keeps its bones, instead of the other way round.
Sled's lashed tight, thanks to you. When the castle road takes you, step where the old slabs show — the sand only pretends to be ground.
Mind the wash pools. Reeds mean shallow; glints mean the wyrm's cousins.
